change_comments.message: Just an initial look. I'll get back to it again.

1 big question really: I want to understand and be sure that our JSON output format has NOT changed, because if we do, we would have a backward incompatible change!
WE might have an integration test that proves it, if not maybe email a before/after output file and/or demo it as part of user story demo!

you can decide to do this as part of this patch or next patch.
also after sourabhs review it will be more clear on what to do.
Overall it looks good. Thanks for your hard work.

inline_comments.message: why is this now a list of maps?!
Update Java doc. is support several queries. Maybe best to refer to the RTD for full details
so this IS needed  to handle null, correct?
NB: number not need if only 1 instance involved in the test
wrong description, this test has nothing to do with including of private properties or not... (and it shouldn't)

does this have to be a valid condition name?!
if yes (diff code path in eth method being tested) you should aslo include a test with an invalid one.
If no, change to 'some condition'

incorrect and misleading
I think someKey:'some Value' wil suffice
as per our previous discussion before (as applied to another test) a more meaningful name to show the behavior might be 'cm handle form query service' and assert exact that on line #280
probably dont need this at all, no need to assert because it is just mocking behavior you are verifying them...
cm handle from the query service
this is only verify what you mocked, so not needed really
not suer if you really need these. is the code you are testing doing anything with these ie do code paths change?!
I think 1 cm handle will do but DO check the id of it e.g 'cm-handle from query service'
There was an issue with openapi early on with this issue. You and Priyank came up with a fix. this was part of the solution
i got an exception if these were not added. therefore i think this is necessary
NB can we pick either PublicCmHandleProperties or CmHandlePublicProperties just to have the type and method the same
Again can we get convention on the naming so it is in the same order everywhere
Do we need to initialise the list size here?
does this mean the output format has changed really or not?
only 1 item is every add so I think this is correct to prevent java from assigning a larger container as it does by default...
OK, I see the new test for invalid conditioname, good!

maybe not needed at all?
Actually if its is it should require yet another test: ... without condition parameters....
'1 *' is for asserting calls not just making calls
We agreed with Toine to handle this issue in a separate commit!
includePrivatePropertiesInQuery - we suffix it with the type of parameter. Its an unsaid convention we are following from the starting.
NB: how abt naming the method as executeCmHandleInventorySearch - just to be in line with the cm handle id searches for inventory?
mapper converts Cm handle without private propertie

Use jsonPath assertions to verify JSON response structure if your mvc supports it, e.g.: 

mvc.perform(get(cmHandleDetailsEndpoint))
   .andExpect(status().isOk())
   .andExpect(jsonPath('$.cmHandle').value('rest version of the cm handle'))
Query CM handle details by DMI service name

and: 'the query service returns a matching CM handle ID'
        def expectedCmHandleId = 'cm-handle from query service'
        partiallyMockedCmHandleQueries.getCmHandleReferencesByDmiPluginIdentifier(_, false) >> [expectedCmHandleId]

and: 'the inventory persistence returns the matching CM handle object'
        mockInventoryPersistence.getYangModelCmHandles([expectedCmHandleId]) >> [
            new YangModelCmHandle(
                id: expectedCmHandleId,
                dmiProperties: [new YangModelCmHandle.Property('name', 'value')],
                publicProperties: []
            )
        ]

description: |
      Executes a query to retrieve CM Handle details that match **all** specified conditions.
      
      - Returns an empty array if no CM Handles match the conditions.
      - You can filter based on any data related to the CM Handle using **CPS Path**.
      - The **ancestor** of the CM Handle is automatically included in the response.

      🔗 For detailed usage:
      - [CM Handle Querying](https://docs.onap.org/projects/onap-cps/en/latest/ncmp-cmhandle-querying.html)
      - [CPS Path Syntax](https://docs.onap.org/projects/onap-cps/en/latest/cps-path.html)
this is goes against the conventions. we never use 'Cm' but 'cm'
the restoutput version is key here
lower case seems to be the convention everywhere else

some of the methods are not found. so i think its not used/set up properly. might not worth the time to refactor the code.
at the other hand the current version more readable.
this is a generated doc. im not sure why is this relevant here. are you sure you commented the right file?
Its my bad I thought its not generated. Thanks for clarification.

yeah, i agree we can change the API (generated) methodname to:
 getPublicCmHandlePropertiesByCmHandleId
this one is in the correct order we just need to change the method name in the API
